description: Class/method/member catalog for the TouchDesigner Python API. Per-method version-introduction (addedIn) data has been intentionally omitted: TouchDesigner's Python class documentation pages do not carry per-method/member 'added in version X' metadata (verified against TOP_Class and App_Class pages), so no granular addedIn claim could be substantiated. Class and member names are retained where verified against the live class pages; descriptions are summaries.
_verificationNote: WP3.5 applied. Every previously-asserted per-method/member 'addedIn' value (e.g. passive=2020, numpyArray=2020, cudaMemory=2021, loadTox-async=2021) and every 'changedIn' record was unverifiable from the authoritative source pages and has therefore been removed (verify-or-omit). Fabricated members App.pythonVersion and App.gpuMemoryUsed were deleted (App class exposes pythonExecutable, not pythonVersion; gpuMemoryUsed is not listed). Entries without an addedIn carry no version claim.

_provenance.fact: Latest TouchDesigner official build is 2025.32820, released 06 May 2026; no 2024 official series exists (timeline goes 2023 -> 2025).
TouchDesigner 2025 official core Python version is 3.11.10.
_provenance.quote: TouchDesigner's core python version has been updated to 3.11.10.

_provenance.fact: POPs (Point Operators) are the flagship new operator family in the 2025 release and the first new operator family in over a decade.
_provenance.quote: This is the first new operator family we've added in over a decade and it promises to be a game changer for the way you work in TouchDesigner!

_provenance.fact: POPs are GPU-based operators that create or modify 3D data, introduced in the 2025.30000 release.
_provenance.quote: Introducing POPs, aka Point Operators! POPs are a new family of operators that run on the GPU and create or modify 3D data.

_provenance.fact: POPs were announced 19 May 2024; DATs (2005) were the previous new operator family.
_provenance.quote: It's not every day we introduce a new operator family for TouchDesigner. DATs (Data Operators) was the last family created in 2005.

_provenance.fact: As of the 2022 release, OpenGL was removed and Vulkan became TouchDesigner's graphics API; the 2022 core Python upgrade was to 3.9.5.
_provenance.quote: The openGL engine used for the last 20 years is gone and TouchDesigner's graphics API is now Vulkan.

_provenance.fact: TouchDesigner 2021 official Python was 3.7.2; the 2021.38110 experimental build upgraded Python to 3.9.5.
_provenance.quote: The version of Python in TouchDesigner has been updated to 3.9.5. The previous version was Python 3.7.2.

_provenance.fact: TouchDesigner Python class docs do not carry per-method version-added metadata; the App class exposes pythonExecutable (not a pythonVersion member).

run.signature: run(script, *args, endFrame=False, fromOP=None, group=None, delayFrames=0, delayMilliSeconds=0, delayRef=me)
run.description: Execute a Python script, optionally with a delay

ops.description: Return multiple operators by path or pattern

passive.description: Return a passive reference that does not cook the operator

fetchAll.description: Fetch a stored value from the local operator or any parent

absTime.description: Absolute frame and seconds since application start

me.description: Reference to the current operator running this script

mod.description: Import a module from a DAT operator

OP.description: Base class for all TouchDesigner operators

copy.description: Copy this operator to another location

destroy.description: Destroy the operator and remove it from the network

op.description: Find an operator relative to this one

parent.description: Return the parent component, n levels up

store.description: Store a value on the operator

addScript.description: Attach a Python callback script to the operator

evalExpression.description: Evaluate a TD expression string in this operator's context

valid.description: True if operator exists and is valid

isCHOP.description: True if operator is a CHOP

isTOP.description: True if operator is a TOP

isDAT.description: True if operator is a DAT

isSOP.description: True if operator is a SOP

isCOMP.description: True if operator is a COMP

isMATERIAL.description: True if operator is a MAT

chan.description: Return a Channel object by index or name

chans.description: Return a list of Channel objects matching patterns

numpyArray.description: Return channel data as a NumPy array

export.description: True if CHOP is being exported

numpyArray.description: Return pixel data as a NumPy array

cudaMemory.description: Return GPU memory handle for CUDA interop

saveByteArray.description: Return pixel data as a bytes object

cell.description: Return a Cell object at the given row and column

cells.description: Return a list of matching Cell objects

row.description: Return a row as a list of Cells

rows.description: Return matching rows as a list

col.description: Return a column as a list of Cells

cols.description: Return matching columns as a list

appendRow.description: Append a new row to the DAT

appendCol.description: Append a new column to the DAT

clear.description: Clear all data from the table

insertRow.description: Insert a row at a specific position

deleteRow.description: Delete a row by name or index

deleteCol.description: Delete a column by name or index

text.description: Full text content of the DAT

pointList.description: Return a list of all Point objects

primList.description: Return a list of all Primitive objects

create.description: Create a new child operator of the given type

findChildren.signature: findChildren(tags=[], maxDepth=1, type=None, name=None, comment=None, path=None, allChildrenFlag=False, parmsMatchingPattern=None, text=None, exact=False)
findChildren.description: Search child operators with flexible filters

saveTox.description: Save this component as a .tox file

addNonCommercialLimit.description: Add a custom non-commercial resolution limit

pythonExecutable.description: Path to the Python executable bundled with this TD build

saveVersion.description: TD version when project was last saved

realTime.description: True if running in real-time mode
